studyName: The Vaginal Microbiome: Disease, Genetics and the Environment

tables.description: This subject phenotype data table includes subject sociodemography (n=5 variables; age, ethnicity/race, education, employment and marital status), affection status, menstrual cycle (n=12 variables), sexual activity (n=3 variables), treatments such as medications, vitamins, hormones, vaccines (n=32 variables), smoking status (n=5 variables), alcohol use (n=6 variables), physical activity (n=6 variables), food intake and diet (n=13 variables), and site where biospecimen were collected/processed. Note from the submitters: "This questionnaire data contains the original uncleaned raw data information collected from the participants during their visits to one of our clinics. The data provided by the participants may contain errors or anomalies which are in some way not fit for use in the analysis. We acknowledge that some fields have nonsensical values. Participants may have accidentally written inappropriate data."

tables.description: This subject sample mapping data table includes a mapping of study subject IDs to sample IDs. Samples are the final preps submitted for genotyping, sequencing, and/or expression data. For example, if one patient (subject ID) gave one sample, and that sample was processed differently to generate 2 sequencing runs, there would be two rows, both using the same subject ID, but having 2 unique sample IDs.

variables.name: sample_ID
variables.description: Unique sample ID

variables.name: subject_ID
variables.description: De-identified subject ID
